description:
Goal: make quoted-source semantic fingerprinting safe and deterministic for whole compiler2 source roots, including runtime-sized module wrappers and their body lists.

Grounding:
- While landing fz-rh2.11.7.10, compiler2's quoted front door successfully parsed runtime prelude and runtime module sources directly to quoted roots.
- Individual items inside runtime modules fingerprint semantically, but fingerprinting the enclosing wrapper/body-list structure for runtime-sized sources like Kernel triggered a SIGBUS during investigation.
- That means parser coverage is moving, but whole-root fingerprint/revision safety is still not proven for the source-authority swap.

Signal:
- QuotedSourceFingerprint walks runtime-sized module wrapper/body-list roots without crashing.
- Tests cover whole-root fingerprinting for representative runtime sources, not just individual items.
- fz-rh2.11.7.9 can rely on whole-root source identity/fingerprint behavior when replacing old AST authority.

Change:
- isolate and fix the unsafe fingerprint walk for wrapper/body-list roots
- add regression tests using runtime-library-sized quoted module roots
- keep the quoted-source substrate single-authority and avoid any fallback bridge

Why:
- source identity/fingerprint is part of the authority story for the next ticket
- if whole quoted roots cannot be fingerprinted safely, compiler2 cannot honestly swap source authority at the code/module level
title: Compiler2 quoted source fingerprint: runtime-sized wrapper/body-list roots must fingerprint safely
